conflicts.description: The manuscript states the marriage date as June 14, 2017 and the year as 2029, yielding approximately 12 years of marriage at story start. However, different chapters use wildly inconsistent values for the marriage duration: 'thirteen years' (Ch 1), 'twelve years' (Ch 2, Ch 17), 'fourteen years' (Ch 7, Ch 10, Ch 18), 'eleven years' (Ch 9, Ch 12). Some references to 'fourteen years' may intend total relationship duration rather than marriage, and Ch 15 explicitly distinguishes 'eighteen years of knowing Mira's face' from marriage. The range from 11 to 14 for 'years of marriage' is too wide and will confuse drafters.
conflicts.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:137 ('thirteen years', Ch 1), :242 and :318 ('twelve years', Ch 2), :1489 ('fourteen years', Ch 7), :1913 ('eleven years of friction', Ch 9), :2210 ('fourteen years of marriage', Ch 10), :2497 ('eleven years', Ch 12), :3746 ('twelve years', Ch 17), :3867 ('fourteen years', Ch 18). Marriage date June 14, 2017 established at :298; year 2029 confirmed by case number NYC-FAM-2029-041187 at :316.
conflicts.canonical_value: Married June 14, 2017. Marriage is approximately 12 years old at story start (late February 2029). Total relationship approximately 14 years (dating from ~2015). Total acquaintance approximately 18 years. Drafters should use 'twelve years' when characters refer to the marriage and 'fourteen years' only when referring to the full relationship.
conflicts.resolution_note: The June 14, 2017 marriage date is deeply embedded in Chapter 2's divorce filing sequence (case number, ceremony details, property listing) and cannot be changed without rewriting that chapter. The math yields ~12 years. References to 'thirteen,' 'eleven,' and 'fourteen years of marriage' are errors. 'Fourteen years' works only as total relationship duration.

conflicts.description: Chapter 2 states the education fund began with '$40/month first deposit' when Theo was born. Chapter 12 states contributions are '$20/week since birth' and calculates 'nine years of contributions' at 'approximately $9,000.' These amounts are inconsistent: $40/month x 108 months = $4,320, while $20/week x 468 weeks = $9,360 (matching the '$9,000' figure).
conflicts.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:308 ('$40/month first deposit', Ch 2), :2619-2621 ('$20/week since birth... approximately $9,000', Ch 12).
conflicts.resolution_note: The $20/week figure is more deeply embedded in Chapter 12's causal structure — the $9,000 total drives the emotional weight of the financial mandate fight and is used in dialogue. Chapter 2's '$40/month first deposit' could be read as the initial deposit amount before the contribution was increased, though this is a charitable interpretation. Canonical value set to $20/week.

conflicts.description: Chapter 13 states Dan catches 'the 8:12 F train from Bergen Street station.' In real and novel NYC geography, Bergen Street station serves the 2/3 lines (Mira's commute), not the F line. Dan's F train station is consistently identified as Seventh Avenue in Chapters 1, 3, 6, and 18.
conflicts.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:437 (Dan at Seventh Avenue F station, Ch 3), :511 (Dan's F line 8:12-8:24 AM, Ch 6 classification report), :2812 ('Bergen Street station' for Dan's F train, Ch 13), :4023 (Dan's F train from Seventh Avenue, Ch 18).
conflicts.canonical_value: Dan takes the F train from Seventh Avenue station. Mira takes the 2/3 from Bergen Street station. These are different stations.
conflicts.resolution_note: Four chapters consistently place Dan at Seventh Avenue for the F train. Chapter 13's 'Bergen Street' is a one-chapter error, likely a drafting slip that swapped the spouses' stations. The station names are tied to real NYC subway geography and cannot be reconciled — Bergen Street does not serve the F line.

conflicts.description: Multiple different teacher names appear for Theo's school across chapters: 'Ms. Okafor' in Room 214 (Ch 4), 'Mrs. Bhandari' teaching thermal expansion (Ch 3), 'Mrs. Achebe' referenced for times tables and allergy rules (Ch 8), and 'Ms. Hollowell' who screams at a gecko (Ch 18). If Theo has one primary classroom teacher, the name is inconsistent. In a school where teachers are proxies, rotating personnel is plausible but not established in the text.
conflicts.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.post_revision.md:415 ('Mrs. Bhandari', Ch 3), :756 ('Ms. Okafor, Room 214', Ch 4), :1626 ('Mrs. Achebe', Ch 8), :3999 ('Ms. Hollowell', Ch 18).
conflicts.canonical_value: Ms. Okafor is Theo's Room 214 teacher (most specifically identified, with physical description and room number). Other names may represent subject-specific teachers, substitute proxies, or drafting inconsistencies.
conflicts.resolution_note: Ms. Okafor in Room 214 is the most embedded reference — she has a physical description, specific behaviors, and a room assignment. Mrs. Bhandari could be a science specialist. Mrs. Achebe and Ms. Hollowell may be math teachers, substitutes, or errors. Future drafters should use Ms. Okafor for Theo's primary teacher unless deliberately establishing that proxy teachers rotate. Note: 'Wendy Okafor' in Chapter 13 is a proxy on Dan's floor at Claude-state — a different character who shares the surname.
